w:r.w:t: If the honey bee disappeared from the surface of the globe the man would only have four years of life left. No more bees, No more pollination, No more plants, No more animals, No more man

Though the honeybees are best known for the honey they produce, their economic role in nature is to pollinate hundreds and thousands of flowering plants and assure seed or fruit set. Honeybees thus play very important role in cross pollinating various agricultural and horticultural crops and increase their yield per unit area and improve their quality. Agricultural scientists in America and Europe have estimated that value of the increased crop yields due to honeybee pollination is several times more than the value of the honey and beeswax the honeybees produce.
About API Culture Technology Center (Bee Keeping)
The Api Culture Technology Center was established in the year 2004 at Rural Technology Park

with NIRD PR and Technical support from AP Bee Keepers’ Association and Kavuri Bee Hive ‘n’ Natural Products. The Api culture center was set up with well-equipped and providing the following services to the Nation.
Trainings on Api. Cuture(Bee Keeping) – for Entrepreneur and Agri. Farmers
Consumer Education about Bee Hive Products
Bee keeping is mainly forest and agro based and decentralized industry. Bee keeping in an

ideal activity for development of a subsidiary occupation providing supplementary income to large number of Agriculture, Horticulturists, Rural, Hilly and Tribal populations because of rich flora available in the areas. The most valuable returns of the industry are the honey, bee-colonies (livestock and other by products and also pollination service rendered by bees, which increases yields at many of the agricultural and horticultural crops.
Honey collection has been known in India since time immemorial and used for religious and medicinal purpose. “Honey” is
produced by honey bees from nectar of flowers or from secretion of the living part of the plants is
being found upon them which the bees gather, transform,
ith specific ingredients, stock and leave to ripen in the cells of the hive. These commodities may be liquid, thick or crystalline.

Honey mainly contains water, glucose, fructose, maltose, sucrose, organic acids, nitrogen, enzymes and vitamins etc. The composition of honey is moisture content, carbohydrates, acids, proteins, mineral and minor components are enzymes, vitamins, pigments, aroma flavor. The average chemical composition and physical properties of honey varies from region to region depending upon floral and climatic conditions and characteristic of the honey.

Honey consumptions being contributed by the house hold sector for daily consumption, religious ceremonies and medicinal purpose. The other market segment constitutes of honey such as manufacturers of pharmaceuticals, Ayurveda medicines, processed food products, baby food, bakery, confectionery and cosmetics

wp:docPr.name: Picture 2
wp:docPr.descr: Image result for bees wax glands

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 2
pic:cNvPr.descr: Image result for bees wax glands

w:r.w:t: Bee wax is said to be the first
known to man. Bees wax used for waterproofing the bottom of the ships. The most important property of
composition. In India 75 percent of bees wax is derived from
percent of the wax is collected from the domesticated hive.

Bee wax is a very complex mixture of more than 200 compounds. Bee wax is very inert material. It is a mixture of saturated Easter, acid and hydrocarbons. Bee wax is very stable material and does not undergo any physical or chemical changes during years of storage.

Uses of bee wax are numerable and in varied fields, from art to medicine and from polishes to ammunition. The trend of use of bee wax in pharmaceuticals in general and in cosmetic in particular, in on the increase and there is a scarcity of pure bees wax in developed countries.

Honey bees collect nectar, convert it into honey and store it as their carbohydrate food. Similarly a single bee colony collects 25 to 40 kg of pollen every year as a source of proteins, vitamins, minerals, oils, and fats etc. Pollen contains all types of nutrients required for the growth young ones in a bee colony and hence considered by many as a ‘complete food’.
There are several hundred flowering plantspecied that provide nectar and pollen of both to the honey bees

. Out of this, the Central Bee Research and Training Institute, as a major nectar and pollen sources of India, have listed about 75 most common plant species. Pollen is mainly used in preparation of pollen supplement and in pharmaceutical and cosmetics industries.

Honey bees collect gummy material secreted by leaf of lower buds of same plants such as poplars, conifers. This substance is known as propolis. Propolis is collected by European honey bees and not by Indian honey bees. Porpolis has antibacterial and anti-fungal properties.

Proplis is used as a folk medicine and for other industrial uses. It was mostly used for Varnishes and Wood preservatives, wound healing, and tooth ache, Throat, Gum and Ear infection and Intestinal disorders.
Royal jelly is a secretion of the glands of the worker honey bees and thus has a very homogeneous appearance,
consistency and composition. Royal jelly is mixture of sugars, proteins and lipids.
Royal jelly is a secretion of the glands of the worker honey bees and thus has a very homogeneous appearance, consistency and composition. Royal jelly is mixture of sugars, proteins and lipids.

The uses of Royal Jelly are varies, even though it is used a food, it is mostly used as an ingredient in many pharmaceutical and cosmetic preparations and also as a food supplement, as an ingredient in food supplements, as an ingredient in medicines, as an ingredient in cosmetics.

wp:docPr.name: Picture 8
wp:docPr.descr: Image result for Bee Venom Powder

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 8
pic:cNvPr.descr: Image result for Bee Venom Powder

w:r.w:t: Bee venom is very complex substance
substance like Proteins as meltitin, apamine, procamin and some petides, enzymes
Amines. Bee Venom is used only in medicines.
The Technology center was supply of Bee Colonies, Queens, Queen Excluders, Feeders, Dummy Boards, Comb Foundation sheets, Extractors, Bee Hives, Hive Tools, Bee viels and necessary
The technology center had a nursery for quality breed and supply the colonies, queens etc. to the Apiarist and Farmers.
training center in associated with National Institute of Rural Development and Panchayati Raj at CIAT- RTP. The center had well equipped display material and training
with deferent types of Bee Hives, Tools and other
. And also the center had Honey Processing & Packing, Wax processing and Comb Foundation making facilities.

The technology center was conducting the different training programmes including Theory and Practical to the entrepreneurs. The center was collecting the nominal fees from the trainees as administration fees.

The technology center was conducting the various skill development, awareness and entrepreneurs developmentprogrammes in associated with various departments like NATIONAL BEE BOARD, ITDA, DRDA, DWAKRA, AGRICULTURE & HORTICULTURE DEPTS.,

Honey bees are good pollinators. The transfer of pollen from anthers to stigma of it or other flower of the same plant or other plant is called pollination. The pollen grains so transferred germinate and send pollen tube through the style to fertilize the ovule in the ovary.
The Pollination can be self-pollination of cross-pollination.

Biotic agents are insects, bird and other animals. Insects play major role in pollination of crops. There is much type of insects, which visit flowers for obtaining nectar and or pollen. Many of these visit flowers just to satisfy own food requirements and such insects are unreliable and inefficient pollinators. There are other, which collect these rewards and store in their nest, this category includes wild bees and true honey bees. Some of the wild bee species like bumble and Mega Chile bees can be used for planned pollination of certain crops but t
honey bees are most suitable for managed pollination of crops.
Many of our Indian crops need cross-pollination. Almost allour oil seeds, vegetables
requires agents like honey bees for fruit or seed set. Bee pollination not only inceases their yield but also improves the quality of the seed and fruit.

Compared to traditional honey, the production of apiary honey is hygienic and price wise it is more costly than wild honey. Apiary honey had market in big cities and pharmaceutical industries, while wild honey finds market in small cities, villages, Ayurvedic preparations etc. Honey is mainly used as a food products, in bakery products and in breweries, cosmetics etc.
